gold_value: lf the Contractor is delayed at any time in the commencement or progress of the Work by adverse weather conditions documented in accordance with Section 15.1.6.2, or other causes beyond the Contractor's control or by other causes that the Contractor asserts, and the Architect determines, justify delay, then the Contract Time shall be extended for such reasonable time as the Architect may determine.

What is the Schedule Requirements Article #?
What are the Schedule Requirements Terms & Conditions?
gold_value: The schedule shall contain detail appropriate for the Project, including (I) the date of commencement of the Work, interim schedule milestone dates, and the date of Substantial Completion; (2) an apportionment of the Work by construction activity; and (3) the time required for completion of each portion of the Work. The schedule shall provide for the orderly progression of the Work to completion and shall not exceed time limits current under the Contract Documents. The schedule shall be revised at appropriate intervals as required by the conditions of the Work and Project.

question: What is the Daily Log & Manpower Article #?
What are the Daily Log & Manpower Terms and Conditions?
What is the Schedule Delay / Time Issues Article #?
What are the Schedule Delay / Time Issues Terms & Conditions?
gold_value:
If the Contractor is delayed at any time in the commencement or progress of the Work by {l) an act or neglect of the Owner or Architect, of an employee of either, or of a Separate Contractor; (2) by changes ordered in the Work; (3) by labor disputes fire, unusual delay in deliveries, unavoidable casualties, adverse weather conditions documented in accordance with Section 15.1.6.2, or other causes beyond the Contractor's control; (4) by delay authorized by the Owner pending mediation and binding dispute resolution; or (5) by other causes that the
Contractor asserts, and the Architect determines, justify delay, then the Contract Time shall be extended for such reasonable time as the Architect may determine.

What are the Schedule Acceleration Directed by Owner Terms & Conditions?
gold_value: The Contractor shall achieve completion by those dates and times (Per the schedule) and shall perform all out-of-sequence work, come-back work, stand-by work, overtime, expediting and acceleration that may be required to complete the Work by those dates and times, all without charge to the Owner.

question: What are the Schedule Definitions of Days (Work / Calendar / Holidays / Etc.)?
gold_value: The term "day" as used in the Contract Documents shall mean calendar day unless otherwise specifically defined.

question: What is the Substantial Completion Article #?
What are the Substantial Completion Terms & Conditions?
gold_value: Subject to adjustments of contract time as provided in the contract documents, the contractor shall achieve substantial completion of the entire work by agreed upon date in executed documents.  If contractors fails, liquidated damages, if any, shall be assessed.

question: What is the Final Completion Definition Article #?
What is the Final Completion Definition?
gold_value: Upon receipt of the Contractor's notice that the Work is ready for final inspection and acceptance and upon receipt of a final Application for Payment the Architect will promptly make such inspection. When the Architect finds the Work acceptable under the Contract Documents and the Contract fully performed, the Architect will promptly issue a final Certificate for Payment stating that to the best of the Architect's knowledge, information and belief, and on the basis of the Architect's on-site visits and inspections, the Work has been completed in accordance with the Contract Documents and that the entire balance found to be due the Contractor and noted in the final Certificate is due and payable.

gold_value: The amount of each progress payment shall include that portion of the Contract Sum properly allocable to materials and equipment delivered and suitably stored at the site for subsequent incorporation in the completed construction, or, if approved in advance by the Owner, suitably stored off the site at a location agreed upon in writing.

question: How many days within Approved Application for Owner Payment?
What is the Article # for IMC's Stop Work Ability for late Owner payment?
What are the Terms & Conditions for IMC's Stop Work Ability for late Owner payment?
gold_value: If the Work is stopped for a period of 60 consecutive days through no act or fault of the Contractor, a Subcontractor, a Sub-subcontractor, or their agents or employees or any other persons or entities performing portions of the Work because the Owner has repeatedly failed to fulfill the Owner's obligations under the Contract Documents with respect to matters important to the progress of the Work, the Contractor may, upon seven additional days' notice to the Owner and the Architect, terminate the Contract and recover from the Owner as provided in 14.1.3.

What is the Warranty Terms & Conditions Article #?
What are the Warranty Terms & Conditions?
gold_value: Work, materials, or equipment not confirming to the standard requirements, including unapproved substitutions, may be considered defective.  The Contractor’s warranty does not cover damage or defect caused by abuse that is not the Contractor’s responsibility, alterations to the Work not executed by the Contractor, improper or insufficient maintenance, improper operation, or normal wear and tear and normal usage. If required by the Owner or Architect, the Contractor shall furnish satisfactory evidence as to the kind and quality of materials and equipment.

question: What is the As-Built / Record Documents Requirements Article #?
What are the As-Built / Record Documents Requirements?
gold_value: The Contractor shall furnish to the Owner two hard copies in three-ring binders and one electronic version on a CD in PDF format (Adobe compatible) of each item identified on the Construction Contract Close Out Checklist, on the form attached to the Agreement (including any Supplement thereto).  If the Contractor does not furnish these items, and the Owner must otherwise obtain such information and data, the costs reasonably incurred by the Owner in doing so will be deducted from payments then or thereafter due to Contractor.

question: What is the Final Payment Article #?
What are the Final Payment Terms & Conditions?
gold_value: Final payment, constituting the entire unpaid balance of the contract sum, shall be made by the owner to the contractor when the contractor has fully performed the contract except the contractor's responsibility to correct work, to satisfy other existing outstanding requirements, and a final certificate for payment has been issued by the architect.

question: What is the Concealed / Unknown / Hazardous Conditions Article #?
What are the Concealed / Unknown / Hazardous Conditions Terms & Conditions?
gold_value: The Contractor is responsible for compliance with any requirements included in the Contract Documents regarding hazardous materials or substances. If the Contractor encounters a hazardous material or substance not addressed in the Contract Documents and if reasonable precautions will be inadequate to prevent foreseeable bodily injury or death to persons resulting from a material or substance, including but not limited to asbestos or polychlorinated biphenyl (PCB), encountered on the site by the Contractor, the Contractor shall, upon recognizing the condition, immediately stop Work in the affected area and notify the Owner and Architect of the condition.
